Thoracic Outlet Syndrome 
URL of this page: https://medlineplus.gov/thoracicoutletsyndrome.html 
Thoracic Outlet Syndrome
Also called: TOS 
Summary 
Thoracic outlet syndrome (TOS) causes pain in the shoulder , arm, and neck. It happens when the nerves or blood vessels just below your neck are compressed, or squeezed. The compression can happen between the muscles of your neck and shoulder or between the first rib and collarbone. You may feel burning, tingling, and numbness along your arm, hand, and fingers. If a nerve is compressed, you may also feel weakn
